author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix silent permission-save failures on custom roles (GH #1597)

The role_level -> role_id migration (2025092004) could silently fail
its combined ALTER TABLE, and 2025092005 then marked it complete
without verifying. Later migrations (2025100102, 2025100501) kept
inserting legacy role_level=0 rows on affected installs, which
collided with the old UNIQUE KEY on custom role permission saves and
got swallowed by INSERT IGNORE with no error surfaced.

Adds a migration that finishes the cleanup: drops the legacy index
and column where still present, purges orphaned legacy rows, dedupes
role_id/permission pairs, and ensures the correct unique key exists.

author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix CSRF via GET-based state-changing endpoints (CWE-352)

The global CSRF gate only fires on non-empty POST requests, but
integrations.php, custom-fields.php, and themes.php performed
destructive actions (delete, toggle, activate) via plain GET
parameters, allowing a malicious page to trigger them on a
logged-in admin's behalf. The upgrade dismiss notice in
updates.messages.php was similarly exposed.

- Convert all state-changing handlers from $_GET to $_POST
- Replace plain <a href> action links with inline POST forms
  carrying the CSRF token, triggering the existing gate
- PRG redirects remain intact; no page refresh risk

Reported responsibly by azesinter.

author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix direct file access bypass on Nginx deployments (CWE-552)

Uploaded files in /upload/files/ were protected only by Apache .htaccess,
which Nginx ignores entirely. On Nginx deployments this allowed unauthenticated
direct access to all uploaded files, bypassing the application's permission model.

- Add nginx.conf.example with the required location block to deny direct access
  to /upload/files/ and other sensitive directories
- Document the required Nginx configuration in README with a prominent warning
- Replace sha1(username) in filename generation with bin2hex(random_bytes(8))
  to eliminate predictable filename enumeration as a secondary attack vector

Reported responsibly by ibnoufikr.

author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix IDOR in user_can_download_file() granting access to any staff role

user_can_download_file() returned true for any logged-in non-Client
role regardless of file ownership, letting restricted staff accounts
download, preview, or serve any file by guessing its numeric id even
though manage-files.php already hid those files from their own
listing. Gate the early-return on the edit_others_files permission
instead, matching the convention already used in manage-files.php
and manage-downloads.php.

Reported by Murodilloyev Toxirjon via SECURITY.md.

author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix 2FA redirect to totp-setup when not required

The legacy authentication_require_email_code option was never cleared by
upgrade_2026032701, so requires2fa() kept returning true even after
two_factor_required was explicitly set to 0 in the new options.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: ignacionelson
message:
Add generic OIDC provider support for SSO (Keycloak, Authentik, Authelia)

Introduces a GenericOidc HybridAuth adapter that auto-discovers endpoints
via {issuer}/.well-known/openid-configuration, making it compatible with
any standards-compliant OIDC server without provider-specific configuration.

Admin settings include: enable toggle, configurable login button label,
issuer URL, client ID/secret, and callback URL. The login page shows a
labelled button when enabled.

Closes #1562

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: ignacionelson
message:
Upgrade HybridAuth to 3.13.0 and migrate Twitter to X

- HybridAuth 3.13.0 adds the X (Twitter v2 OAuth2) provider, OktaOIDC,
  fixes CVE-2026-4587, and improves PHP 8.4/8.5 compatibility
- Twitter (OAuth1/v1.1 API) was fully deprecated and non-functional;
  replaced with X provider using new x_signin_enabled/x_client_id/x_client_secret
  options — existing OAuth1 credentials are not migrated as they are incompatible
- Admin UI notes that new OAuth2 credentials are required from developer.twitter.com

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: ignacionelson
message:
Enforce TOTP setup before granting access when 2FA is required

When the admin requires two-factor authentication and a user hasn't
configured TOTP yet, they are now logged in and immediately redirected
to totp-setup.php instead of falling back to email-based codes. The
new totp_setup_required() middleware runs in both the admin header and
the client template to cover all entry points.

Closes #1552

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix reflected XSS in thumbnails-regenerate.php via date filter params

The start_date and end_date GET parameters were echoed into HTML input
value attributes without escaping, allowing arbitrary script injection.
Applied htmlspecialchars(ENT_QUOTES, UTF-8) at the point of input reading.

Reported by: Sehwang Kim (kimboann) @ AhnLab, Inc.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix large file download cutoff and add LiteSpeed support

Increase PHP download chunk size from 8KB to 2MB and use echo instead
of print to prevent memory accumulation that caused downloads to cut off
at the memory_limit threshold (e.g. 1GB). Also add LiteSpeed as a
download method option, sending the X-LiteSpeed-Location header for
native server-level file serving.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix TOTP setup failing on upgrades from older versions (#1546)

Installations upgraded from older versions (e.g. r1945) don't have
ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY in sys.config.php, causing a fatal error when
setting up TOTP 2FA. Both Totp and Encryption classes now auto-generate
and persist the key to the config file when it's missing.

Also fixes PHP 8.4 deprecation warnings:
- Explicit nullable types for Auth and RememberMe constructors
- Null safety in i18n MO file header parsing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: ignacionelson
message:
Fix LDAP auto-create users failing due to permission check (#1545)

During LDAP login, no user is authenticated yet, so the permission
check in Users::create() always fails. Add an LDAP bypass flag
(mirroring the existing self-registration pattern) that skips the
permission check when creating users via LDAP auto-create.
Also cleans up debug error_log calls from createFromLdap().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: ignacionelson
message:
Add in-app changelog viewer on database upgrade notice

When the database is updated after a version upgrade, a "See what's
new in rXXXX" link now opens a Bootstrap modal that fetches and renders
the corresponding assets/changelogs/{version}.md file via marked.js.

- changelogModal.js: new part — lazy-fetches and renders the MD file on
  first open, shows a spinner while loading
- _changelog-modal.scss: styles for the trigger link and rendered markdown
- updates.messages.php: restructured db_upgraded line; added modal HTML
  and larger "See what's new" link on a separate line
- assets/changelogs/r1994.md, r2002.md: real changelogs sourced from git
  history and WHATS_NEW.md
- marked added to package.json and bundled into assets.js via gulpfile.js

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
